arg.name: robot_name
arg.description: Name of the robot or application for unique identification.

arg.name: prefix
arg.description: Prefix for the robot. If you have multiple robots in the same namespace, you can use this to distinguish them.

arg.name: hardware_is_async
arg.description: If true, the hardware interface will be asynchronous. If false, it will be synchronous.

arg.name: configuration_package
arg.description: Package with configuration files, e.g., controllers, initial positions.

arg.name: initial_positions_file
arg.description: YAML file with the initial positions when using mock hardware from ros2_control. Robots using '_all_zeros' file are - kr210l150 -

arg.name: description_package
arg.description: Description package with robot URDF/xacro files.

arg.name: description_macro_file
arg.description: URDF/XACRO description file with of the robot or application. The expected location of the file is description_package/urdf/.

arg.name: use_eki_communication
arg.description: Use EKI communication to the KUKA Robot Controller (KR-C). If the flag is set to true 'eki_robot_ip' and 'eki_robot_port' arguments define the endpoint of robot controller to connect.

arg.name: eki_robot_ip
arg.description: IP address for the robot controller for communication using EKI protocol.

arg.name: eki_robot_port
arg.description: Port by which the robot can be reached for communication using EKI protocol.

arg.name: use_rsi_communication
arg.description: Use RSI communication to the KUKA Robot Controller (KR-C). If the flag is set to true 'rsi_listen_ip' and 'rsi_robot_port' arguments define the endpoint where robot controller should connect to. (Keep in mind that you have to define this endpoint also in the RSI configuration of the program.)

arg.name: rsi_listen_ip
arg.description: IP address of a computer where robot controller should connect for communication using RSI protocol.

arg.name: rsi_listen_port
arg.description: Port on a computer where robot controller should connect for communication using RSI protocol.

arg.name: use_mock_hardware
arg.description: Start robot with robot with fake hardware mirroring command to its states.

let.name: robot_description_content
let.value: $(command '$(find-exec xacro) $(find-pkg-share kuka_ros2_control_support)/urdf/common_kuka.xacro robot_name:=$(var robot_name) prefix:=$(var prefix) is_async:=$(var hardware_is_async) description_package:=$(var description_package) description_macro_file:=$(var description_macro_file) use_eki_communication:=$(var use_eki_communication) eki_robot_ip:=$(var eki_robot_ip) eki_robot_port:=$(var eki_robot_port) use_rsi_communication:=$(var use_rsi_communication) rsi_listen_ip:=$(var rsi_listen_ip) rsi_listen_port:=$(var rsi_listen_port) use_mock_hardware:=$(var use_mock_hardware) initial_positions_file:=$(find-pkg-share $(var configuration_package))/config/$(var initial_positions_file)')
